title: macroptilium fraternum
intro: Macroptilium fraternum, also known as hairy trefoil, is a perennial legume native to the Americas. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and as an antiseptic agent. The plant is rich in flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds that have been associated with various health benefits, such as antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help to lower cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health. Macroptilium fraternum can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
